content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 958

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Int
(916) 322-5662            322-5660              322-5901              322-6441              322-5444


March 3, 1982


Mr. Stephen Lovas
26 Rollingwood Drive
Rolling Hills Estates, CA  90274

Dear Mr. Lovas:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you during our telephone
conversations of February 4, 1982, and February 17, 1982.

During both conversations you asked how you, as a candidate for
city council, would report payments using your own money for any
campaign materials or ads that would include the names of other
candidates, even though this action by you would be completely at
your own discretion and without the knowledge or approval of the
other candidates.

I advised you on both occasions that you would report any and all
payments on Form 430, Candidate's Campaign Statement since you wil
not have a committee. You are required to report your own money a
distributions received on Schedule A and all payments made by you
will be reported on Schedule E. I also stated that, for clarifica
tion, you should make a notation on Schedule E to indicate that a
particular payment was for a piece of literature or advertisement
which also endorsed the other candidates.

I hope this information is helpful. If I can be of any further
assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]

Helen P. Arriola
Political Reform Consultant

HPA:cm

Stephen Lucas
20 Slingawood Drive
Rolling Hills Estates, California 90274
February 7, 1982

Ms. Helen Ariola
Fair Political Practice Commission
107 S. Broadway, Room 700
Los Angeles, Ca. 90012

Dear Ms. Ariola,

I'm afraid I didn't explain too well what my problem
was when I called you recently so it may be well to
write it out for leisurely but careful consideration.

I should appreciate guidance in connection with the
filing of campaign statement. I plan to run for
city council in Rolling Hills Estates. Two other
gentlemen will also be running who hold views similar
to my own with respect to city government. I intend to
spend over $500 to win the election. I will have no
committee, no treasurer and will spend as much as I
feel appropriate, when I think best during the campaign,
solely at my discretion. Because we are kindred spirits,
I should like to include the names of the two gentlemen
running, who share my views. These two -eople will have
no voice in the signing, wording or funding of whatever
advertising or campaign literature I may choose to disseminate and will contribute nothing toward the effort
I will personally and exclusively control.

In such a situation, what type of reporting will fulfill
the requirements of the Campaign Disclosure Provision
of the Political Reform Act? The instructions and information I have read so far do not seem to cover the
variation of a race filed with. According to a
telephone call, with following confirming letter
would be most helpful to get me started quickly and stay
strictly within your rules and regulations. My telephone
number is 375-5155.

Many thanks for your early assistance.

embedding.qa_text: March 3, 1982 Mr. Stephen Lovas 26 Rollingwood Drive Rolling Hills Estates, CA 90274 Dear Mr. Lovas: This letter is to confirm my advice to you during our telephone conversations of February 4, 1982, and February 17, 1982. During both conversations you asked how you, as a candidate for city council, would report payments using your own money for any campaign materials or ads that would include the names of other candidates, even though this action by you would be completely at your own discretion and without the knowledge or approval of the other candidates. I advised you on both occasions that you would report any and all payments on Form 430, Candidate's Campaign Statement since you will not have a committee. You are required to report your own money a contributions received on Schedule A and all payments made by you will be reported on Schedule E. I also stated that, for clarification, you should make a notation on Schedule E to indicate that a particular payment was for a piece of literature or advertisement which also endorsed the other candidates. I hope this information is helpful. If I can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, Jeanne Pitchard for Helen P. Arriola Political Reform Consultant Stephen Lucas 20 Rollingwood Drive Rolling Hills Estates, California 90274 February 7, 1982 Ms. Helen Ariola Fair Political Practice Commission 107 S. Broadway, Room 7007 Los Angeles, Ca. 90012 Dear Ms. Ariola, I'm afraid I didn't explain too well what my problem was when I called you recently so it may be well to write it out for leisurely but careful consideration. I should appreciate guidance in connection with the filing of campaign statements. I plan to run for city council in Rolling Hills Estates. Two other gentlemen will also be running who hold views similar to my own with respect to city government. I intend to spend over $500 to win the election. I will have no committee, no treasurer and will spend as much as I feel appropriate, when I think best during the campaign, solely at my discretion. Because we are kindred spirits, I should like to include the names of the two gentlemen running, who share my views. These two people will have no voice in the timing, wording or funding of whatever advertising or campaign literature I may choose to disseminate and will contribute nothing toward the effort I will personally and exclusively control. In such a situation, what type of reporting will fulfil the requirements of the Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act? The instructions and information I have read so far do not seem to cover the particular situation I am faced with. Accordingly, a telephone call, with following confirming letter would be most helpful to get me started quickly and stay strictly within your rules and regulations. My telephone number is 373-5165. Many thanks for your early assistance.
